rationale.R1.2: The section constructs a causal chain: selective gate → down-weighting unreliable cells → robustness → mAP gains; the ablation is cited as confirming evidence. Logical consistency of this chain and whether the single ablation step adequately supports the causal claim are central to a scientific review.
rationale.R1.3: The section's core is a set of quantitative performance claims (89.2/81.4 vs 88.7/80.6, 0.4-point ablation drop, 1.6-point single-agent gap). Whether the reported numbers are internally coherent, whether the described experimental protocol produces the stated metrics, and whether the concluding 'leading approach' claim is proportionate to the evidence are load-bearing for any reading of 'review.'
rationale.R1.6: Without a qualifier, 'review this section' could be primarily a prose-quality request. Sentence-level overclaims ('establish MARCO as the leading approach'), vague attributions ('We attribute this improvement to…'), and paragraph cohesion are all plausible primary targets.

parsed.clarification_question: Should this review prioritize scientific/technical validity — checking whether the quantitative claims are adequately supported by the reported evidence and whether the causal inferences (selective gate → robustness) hold — or writing/presentation quality, focused on prose clarity, precision of language, and structural flow?
